name: Credit Counseling Of Arkansas
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling agency offering free budget, credit, and housing counseling plus debt management programs since 1995.
description_long:
Credit Counseling of Arkansas (CCOA), Inc. has operated as a non-profit organization since 1995, serving thousands of families nationwide with financial guidance and debt solutions. The organization is HUD-certified for housing counseling and appears to be affiliated with the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC), evidenced by their client receiving an NFCC Brighter Financial Future award.

CCOA offers free, no-obligation financial consultations and counseling services across multiple areas: budget counseling, credit report reviews, debt management programs, pre-filing bankruptcy counseling, first-time homebuyer counseling, mortgage delinquency counseling, and reverse mortgage counseling. They claim their debt management program can reduce interest rates, stop fees, and halt collection calls. The organization provides free financial education resources and seminars, with counseling available via phone, in-person, or email (for budget and debt management only).

The company distinguishes itself through certified financial counselors, multiple physical locations in Arkansas (Fayetteville, Bentonville, Fort Smith), and a client-focused approach emphasizing personal relationships. Their reported client outcomes are notable: 93% report better control of finances, 94% use information from classes in their financial practices, and 97% report being closer to becoming debt-free. They accept clients nationwide despite having physical offices in Arkansas.

As a free-help organization, CCOA is appropriate for consumers seeking legitimate, non-profit financial counseling without cost barriers. The main caveat is that debt management programs, while non-predatory, still require commitment to a structured repayment plan, and results depend on individual circumstances and adherence to the counseling plan.
pros: Completely free financial consultations and budget counseling with no obligation
HUD-certified housing counseling agency covering homebuying, delinquency, and reverse mortgages
Non-profit status ensures no predatory lending or hidden fees in debt management programs
Multiple service delivery options: in-person, phone, or email consultations
Certified financial counselors providing personalized guidance
Debt management program claims to reduce interest rates and stop collection calls
Serves clients nationwide despite Arkansas-based offices; established 30+ year track record
cons: Limited physical locations (only 3 offices in Arkansas); may require phone consultations for out-of-state clients
Debt management program enrollment requires strict budget adherence and commitment to repayment plans, not suitable for all situations
Requires government-issued photo ID for credit report reviews, adding a verification step
No pricing transparency on website for any services beyond stating they are 'free'
No clear information about whether they offer bankruptcy filing assistance or only pre-filing counseling
best_for: Families struggling with credit card, medical, or housing debt seeking legitimate non-profit guidance
First-time homebuyers needing HUD-certified counseling before purchase
Homeowners facing mortgage delinquency or considering reverse mortgages
Individuals seeking free financial education and budgeting skills improvement
diagnosis: CCOA is best for consumers seeking free, legitimate non-profit credit counseling and debt management from certified professionals without fear of predatory practices. The main caveat is that their debt management programs require sustained commitment to structured repayment plans and budget discipline—they provide guidance and negotiation, but cannot eliminate debt or guarantee specific outcomes.
services: Free budget counseling and personalized financial planning
Credit report reviews and credit improvement advice from certified counselors
Debt Management Program with potential interest rate reduction and fee elimination
Free financial seminars and education resources
Credit card, medical, and housing debt assistance
Financial consultations via phone, in-person, or email
Client online portal (Client Login and Web Payment access)
Newsletter subscription for financial education updates
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1111 E Zion Rd, N/A, Fayetteville, AR 72703
